title: Smarter SMSF improves education course
full_text: NEWS COMPLIANCE, CONTRIBUTIONS, EDUCATION, ESTATE PLANNING, TAX Smarter SMSF improves education course May 5, 2026 Darin Tyson-Chan Smarter SMSF has upgraded its education offering to include all of the latest changes to the superannuation and tax legislation. Add us as a preferred source on Google Specialist service provider Smarter SMSF has enhanced the curriculum of one of its education options to include all of the superannuation and tax legislation changes up to April 2026. The changes have been made to the Smarter education tier of the firm’s SMSF Essentials Course, which has seen a complete redesign with an emphasis on high interaction with students. To this end, users will now be able to gain advantage from a variety of new instructional videos and an overall enhanced multi-media experience. The upgrade to the educational package has resulted in the SMSF Essentials Course now being able to provide practitioner users with 25 accredited continuing professional development (CPD) hours. Previously the course carried 18 accredited CPD hours. Smarter SMSF chief executive and co-founder Aaron Dunn noted the initiative was needed to allow the best possibility for practitioners to navigate the amount of change the sector is currently witnessing. “The jump to 25 CPD hours is a direct response to the increasing complexity practitioners face daily. We haven’t just added more content; we’ve refined what is ‘essential’ to ensure that every minute spent in this course translates into practical competency,” Dunn explained. With regard to some of the more specific amendments, Module 1 has been completely redesigned. The module provides insights into understanding the SMSF definition and how a practitioner must operate in today’s legislative and regulatory environment, and covers subjects such as estate planning, reporting obligations, taxation, contributions, investment rules, and pensions and drawdowns. Smarter SMSF technical and education manager Tim Miller pointed out the updates are of a very practical nature. “Education should never be static and the legislation continues to move,” Miller said. “This updated version of the course to April 2026 ensures that students are not only up to date with the super and tax laws, but are practically equipped with materials that are built to help them to bridge the gap between technical knowledge and application with your SMSF clients.” The improved course is now available on the organisation’s platform and students will also receive a complimentary period of SMSF Association membership as an associate. X linkedin Darin Tyson-Chan EDITOR Darin is the founder and publisher of Benchmark Media, an independent publishing house whose mastheads include financialobserver, selfmanagedsuper and smstrusteenews. He has been covering the financial services space for over a decade and in this time he has worked for a variety of trade publications. Darin has also written for the Money section published in The Sydney Morning Herald and The Age. Articles by Darin PREVIOUS ARTICLE NEXT ARTICLE
summary: Smarter SMSF has revamped its SMSF Essentials Course to incorporate the latest superannuation and tax legislation changes up to April 2026. The course now offers 25 accredited CPD hours, up from 18, and features enhanced multimedia content for better student engagement. This update aims to equip practitioners with practical knowledge to navigate the complexities of the evolving regulatory environment.

heading: Superannuation and Investments
page_title: Smarter SMSF improves education course | SMS Magazine
key_points: The SMSF Essentials Course has been redesigned to include recent legislative changes.
Participants can now earn 25 accredited CPD hours, an increase from the previous 18 hours.
The course focuses on practical application of knowledge for SMSF practitioners.
relevance: This is relevant to actuaries and financial services professionals as it highlights the importance of ongoing education and adaptation to regulatory changes in the superannuation sector.
